name: Ten
description_short: TENCU is a member-owned credit union serving Middle Tennessee with 8 branch locations, offering personal loans, auto financing, mortgages, and deposit accounts since 1950.
description_long:
Ten Credit Union (TENCU) was founded in 1950 and has grown to serve members across Middle Tennessee with physical branches in Nashville, Cookeville, Bellevue, Rivergate, Cool Springs, Gallatin, and Johnson City. As a federally-insured credit union regulated by the NCUA, TENCU operates as a not-for-profit member-owned institution, meaning members are technically owners rather than customers. The credit union celebrated its 75th annual membership meeting, indicating a stable, long-established organization with deep community roots.

TENCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal loans (as low as 10.99% APR), auto loans (as low as 5.49% APR), mortgage loans, credit cards (Platinum Visa at 14.15% APR), and deposit products like checking, savings, money market accounts, and share certificates (3.65% APY). Members can access wealth management services through Bethel Coleman, CRPC, student loan products, and insurance services including auto and home insurance. Digital banking is available through online banking, mobile banking, and video banking platforms with 24/7 access.

TENCU distinguishes itself through its membership model, which theoretically provides better rates and lower fees than for-profit banks, and its emphasis on financial education through free Money IQ resources. The credit union maintains multiple physical branches and shared branch/ATM networks, providing members with widespread in-person access. They actively engage members through community initiatives and offer tools like credit score tracking, financial calculators, and a "Switch to TENCU" program for account transfers.

However, as a regional credit union, TENCU's reach is limited to Middle Tennessee, making it inaccessible to non-members in other areas. While rates are competitive, they are not exceptionally low compared to other credit unions or online lenders. The website provides limited detail on membership requirements, eligibility criteria, or specific underwriting standards, which may affect accessibility for some consumers.
services: Mortgage loans for home purchase and refinance
Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Online and mobile banking with bill pay
pros: Member-owned credit union structure typically offers lower rates and fewer fees than for-profit banks
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.49% APR and personal loans at 10.99% APR
Eight physical branch locations across Middle Tennessee plus shared branch and ATM network access
NCUA federal insurance protecting deposits up to $250,000 per account
Free financial education through Money IQ program
Established 75-year history (since 1950) indicating stability and community trust
Comprehensive services including wealth management, insurance, and investment products in one institution
cons: Geographic limitation: only serves Middle Tennessee region, restricting membership and accessibility
Limited transparency on membership eligibility requirements and qualification standards on website
Personal loan rates starting at 10.99% APR are moderate but not exceptionally competitive compared to some national online lenders
Website provides minimal detail on specific loan terms, approval timelines, or underwriting processes
No information about credit builder loans or specialized products for members with poor credit history
best_for: Middle Tennessee residents seeking lower-cost banking and lending through a member-owned cooperative structure
Auto buyers and borrowers wanting competitive rates with local branch support and personal service
Members prioritizing stability and community connection over seeking the absolute lowest rates nationally
diagnosis: TENCU is best for Middle Tennessee residents who value the member-owned cooperative model and want to consolidate banking, lending, and investment services with a stable, community-focused institution. The main caveat is geographic limitation—non-residents cannot join, and rates, while competitive, are not necessarily lower than national online alternatives or other credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Ten — Credit Union in NASHVILLE, TN | CreditDoc
meta_description: Ten — NCUA-insured credit union in NASHVILLE, TN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
